The ideal candidate will provide strategic leadership, oversee shelter operations and community programs, and ensure the organization continues to fulfill its mission with excellence and integrity.

This leadership role requires a dynamic professional possessing strong management skills, with experience in donor recruitment and retention a plus.

● Provide overall leadership and strategic direction for the organization, ensuring alignment with SOCKS’ mission, values, and goals

**Job Title:** Executive Director

**Reports To:** Board of Directors

**FLSA Status:** Full\-Time Salaried/Exempt

**Salary range:** $110,000 \- $125,000, commensurate with experience

**Benefits:** Medical, Dental, Vision, Life, 403 (b) Retirement Plan, Paid Vacation, and more

**About the Organization** 

Lafayette Habitat for Humanity, Inc. (LHFH) is a well\-respected Louisiana nonprofit organization that has been providing affordable housing and home repairs to families in need for over 34 years in the Acadiana area. Since 1992, the organization has built and mortgaged over 160 homes and has helped many families achieve and sustain their dream of homeownership. See https://habitatlafayette.org/

The LHFH Board of Directors seeks exceptional candidates for the role of Executive Director with the ability to lead one of the most productive affiliates of Habitat for Humanity in the state of Louisiana. Lafayette Habitat for Humanity serves the greater Lafayette area with first\-time homeownership and home preservation programs, including new home construction, homeownership counseling, mortgage financing and loan servicing, critical home repairs for owner\-occupied homes, and support for resident\-led neighborhood revitalization initiatives in Lafayette’s urban core. LHFH also operates a ReStore, which provides a source of affordable home goods and building materials as well as operating revenue for our core homeownership programs. LHFH recognizes the value all these mission\-critical services provide as the stock of affordable housing has continued to age in the area and access to affordable housing intersects with so many other community issues including homelessness.

**About Our Community** 

Lafayette sits at the heart of the Acadiana region in south Louisiana, a community rich in history, culture, music, food, and French Cajun \& Creole influence. Lafayette has been described as the “Happiest City in America,” the “Best Food City in America,” and the “Cajun and Zydeco Capital of the World.” Lafayette and Acadiana have a deep cultural identity and a warm community spirit that values families, friendships, and a genuine *joie de vivre.* Lafayette is also a growing community with a vibrant economic outlook. The Lafayette Economic Development Authority (LEDA) reports that “\[within the last] *two years, four companies announced the creation of more than 1,100 technology jobs in Lafayette, cementing the region’s reputation as a technology hub in the South.”* 

**Mission \& Vision** 

**Mission:** Seeking to put God’s love into action, Habitat for Humanity brings people together to build homes, communities and hope.

**Vision:** A world where everyone has a decent place to live.

**Our Principles:** 

Demonstrate the love of Jesus Christ

Focus on shelter

Advocate for affordable housing

Promote dignity and hope

Support sustainable and transformative development

**Position Summary** 

The Executive Director reports directly to the Board of Directors of Lafayette Habitat for Humanity, Inc. and is responsible for overall organizational performance, long\-term sustainability, real estate and construction strategy, fund development, community partnerships, staff leadership, and governance compliance. This role requires a dynamic, highly relational leader who can operate at both strategic and tactical levels – guiding the affiliate through real estate development, capital planning, and organizational scaling with a community\-centered ministry mindset to further impact in alignment with the mission of the organization.

The Executive Director accepts the Affiliate Covenant of Habitat for Humanity International (HFHI), serves as a leading advocate for Habitat’s vision and mission, and works alongside the Board of Directors to develop and implement strategic and operational plans. This includes integrating the work of committees, staff, and volunteers into a coherent, consistent, and effective program of advocacy, construction, financing, and family support.

**Duties \& Responsibilities** 

**Strategic Planning, Program Development \& Administration** 

Supervise, hire, and provide direction to the staff responsible for all functions of Lafayette Habitat, including new home construction, home preservation, ReStore, neighborhood revitalization, volunteer management, homeowner services, mortgage services, site selection, faith relations, marketing, and administration

Provide leadership in developing program, organizational, and financial plans with the Board of Directors and staff, and execute plans and policies as directed by the Board

Establish and maintain effective working relationships with all governmental agencies necessary for Lafayette Habitat to successfully conduct its programs

Ensure that all required official records and documents are maintained, and ensure compliance with federal, state, and local regulations and guidelines

Maintain a working knowledge of significant developments and trends within the greater Lafayette area and the broader Habitat community

Ensure the affiliate operates in accordance with Habitat for Humanity International's (HFHI) guidelines, standards, and mission

Promote active and broad participation by volunteers in all areas of the organization’s work

**Board Relations** 

Serve as an advisor to the Board and support effective governance

Collaborate with the Board to define and articulate the affiliate’s vision and strategic plan

Assist the Board Chair in setting agendas for Board and committee meetings

Assist the Nominating Committee with recruiting qualified community members to the Board

Provide new Board member orientation and ongoing development opportunities

Keep the Board of Directors fully informed on the financial and organizational health of Lafayette Habitat and the important factors influencing it

**Fundraising \& Revenue Diversification**

jobs.title: Managing Director
jobs.description:
### **About Us**



Orr Group is a strategic partner to nonprofit organizations, helping them solve some of the world's most pressing challenges. Through our embedded partnership model, we go beyond traditional consulting \- providing hands\-on leadership, expertise, and execution to drive transformative fundraising and organizational growth. For over 30 years, we have collaborated with mission\-driven organizations to design and implement innovative fundraising strategies, grow revenue, and build capacity to ensure long\-term success. Our work spans campaign management, major gifts strategy, board development, planned giving, talent management, and beyond. We remain nimble, forward\-thinking, and results\-driven, helping our clients navigate an ever\-evolving philanthropic landscape.



Orr Groups has offices based in Washington, DC, New York City, and Chicago, and will consider candidates nationwide. Washington, New York, and Chicago represent the firm's primary client relationships and greatest near\-term opportunities. Candidates in other major markets will be considered where there is a demonstrated existing network and documented market opportunity. The ideal candidate brings established senior relationships they can activate from day one and the flexibility to travel up to 25% to serve clients across geographies.


### **Position Summary**



Managing Directors at Orr Group are responsible for driving business development through sourcing, securing, growing, and retaining client relationships for the firm. They partner with the Growth Team to cultivate relationships that deploy Orr Group's services across the sector — serving as the firm's most senior external face and primary revenue drivers.



This is an entrepreneurial role for a self\-starter with a demonstrated ability to independently build and manage a significant book of business. The ideal candidate brings an established network of nonprofit leaders, a proven record of generating revenue through consultative relationship development, and the credibility to serve as a trusted advisor to CEOs, development executives, and boards. Experience as a Chief Development Officer or equivalent senior nonprofit leader, along with firsthand leadership of a major capital or comprehensive campaign, is strongly preferred.



Managing Directors manage a combination of regional and national clients. Depending on client needs, this role may require up to 25% travel or time working at a client site. Candidates based in Washington, DC or New York City are strongly preferred. Candidates in other major markets nationwide will be considered where there is a demonstrated network and documented market opportunity.


### **Responsibilities**



Managing Directors are expected to arrive with the relationships, credibility, expertise, and drive to build a robust client portfolio. While the Growth Team provides strategic partnership and support, new MDs should expect to develop their pipeline independently in the first year.



Responsibilities include:


* Own the full business development lifecycle from identifying prospects and cultivating relationships to negotiating and closing new client engagements.
* Independently build and manage a client pipeline through personal networks, targeted outreach, and market presence.
* Meet or exceed annual business development and revenue goals through new client acquisition and expansion of existing relationships.
* Secure and expand C\-suite and board\-level relationships across significant nonprofits, leveraging both self\-sourced opportunities and Growth Team support.
* Drive client retention through proactive relationship management, delivering measurable satisfaction and long\-term engagement.
* Become knowledgeable about all the firm's Practice Areas in order to identify prospective client needs and position appropriate services and expertise to address them.
* Identify and design the specific service or combination of services a client will engage Orr Group to provide.
* Maintain accountability for overall client profitability, satisfaction, and retention.
* Coordinate with and provide feedback to the Practice Area Leads to strengthen service offerings and identify emerging market opportunities.
* Build a personal brand as a thought leader and expert in one or more areas of Orr Group's targeted expertise, representing Orr Group through speaking engagements, professional associations, writing, and industry events.
* Champion innovative, efficient ways of working across the team, including the thoughtful use of AI to strengthen client service, sharpen strategic thinking, and improve how Orr Group delivers value.


Managing Directors may also serve as the leader on client projects to ensure high\-quality client service delivery. Generally, MDs provide executive oversight and management of the client relationship and ensure that services are delivered on time, in scope, and on budget.



Additional responsibilities include but are not limited to:


* Communicating effectively with a wide variety of audiences, including board members, C\-suite, staff, donors, and outside advisors.
* Providing high\-level oversight of client engagement teams, scopes, and service delivery.
* Serving as mentor and coach to team members.
* Presenting strategic recommendations and distilling complex analyses into actionable insights.
* Discovering and employing new and innovative solutions that create value for clients and strengthen Orr Group's competitive position.

### **Qualifications**


* Bachelor's degree required.
* 10\+ years of nonprofit sector experience, with significant exposure to fundraising strategy, executive leadership, or nonprofit consulting at a senior level.
* Prior CDO, VP of Development, or equivalent senior in\-house fundraising leadership strongly preferred.
* Demonstrated track record of independently sourcing and closing new business and consistently achieving revenue goals.
* Experience managing or lea

jobs.title: Director of Development, Corporate & Foundation Relations, UF Health
jobs.description:
**JOB NO:**


540591  

**WORK TYPE:**


Staff Full\-Time  

**LOCATION:**


Main Campus (Gainesville, FL)  

**CATEGORIES:**


Advancement  

**DEPARTMENT:**


29011500 \- MD\-ALUMNI AFFAIRS  


**CLASSIFICATION TITLE:**

DIR, Development

  

  

**CLASSIFICATION MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS:**

Master's degree in appropriate area of specialization and three years of appropriate experience in Development or a related field; or a bachelor's degree in appropriate areas of specialization and five years of experience in Development or a related field.

  

  

**JOB DESCRIPTION:**

The Director of Development for Corporate and Foundation Relations is a key member of the UF Health Advancement team, responsible for securing philanthropic support through strategic corporate and foundation partnerships. Reporting to the Executive Director of Development, this role manages a portfolio of prospects, develops compelling funding proposals, and collaborates with faculty, researchers, administrators, and advancement colleagues to align funding opportunities with institutional priorities. This is an exciting opportunity for a relationship\-driven fundraising professional to build impactful partnerships, advance innovative programs, and help drive meaningful change across UF Health. Join us in creating powerful partnerships that fuel innovation, expand opportunity, and strengthen the future of health care, research, and education.



Candidates located in major metropolitan cities across North and Central Florida (Tampa, Orlando, Jacksonville, Tallahassee) may be considered.


**Corporate \& Foundation Portfolio Management**


* **Relationship Management:** Build and manage a portfolio of corporate and foundation prospects of all capacities with a focus on proposals $100,000\+. Cultivate and maintain strong relationships with corporate and foundation representatives, including regular communication, meetings, and stewardship activities to ensure ongoing support and engagement. Seek guidance from the Executive Director for proposals at or above $500,000\.
* **Proposal Development:** Identify, cultivate, and solicit corporate and foundation prospects that align with the needs, priorities, and research of UF Health (including all six colleges, centers \& institutes, and hospital campuses). Lead the development of compelling grant proposals, sponsorship requests, and funding applications tailored to the priorities and interests of potential corporate and foundation partners, ensuring alignment with their giving guidelines and objectives.
* **Collaboration:** Collaborate closely with faculty, researchers, administrators, and other stakeholders within the UF Health Colleges to identify funding needs and opportunities, develop funding strategies, and ensure alignment between donor interests and institutional priorities.
* **Donor Recognition:** Identify, coordinate, and steward new and existing corporate and foundation relationships. Develop and implement strategies for recognizing and acknowledging corporate and foundation donors, including naming opportunities, plaques, publications, and other forms of recognition as appropriate.


**Project Management**


* **Grant Management:** Draft, edit, and submit proposals and other funding opportunities according to the requirements and processes established for each individual funding institution. Oversee the process, including proposal development, reporting, compliance, and acknowledgment, to ensure the fulfillment of grant requirements and maintain strong relationships with funders. Ensure timely action within frequent and firm deadlines unique to each proposal and ensuing funding.
* **Data Management:** Actively monitor proposal progression, including, but not limited to, processing letters of agreement, tracking proposals from submission to decision, meeting post\-funding reporting deadlines, and responding to funding institution requests for additional information as necessary.
* **Events and Engagement:** Coordinate and oversee campus visits for corporate and foundation representatives, including, but not limited to, engaging key stakeholders to secure participation, building itineraries, and building customized experiences for visitors that enhance their relationship with the University community.
* Serve as a primary point of contact for funding institutions when necessary and provide reports as requested.
* Act as an intermediary between UF Health faculty/physicians and funding institutions as necessary, ensuring that all appropriate stakeholders are aware of and engaged in the proposal process.
* Proactively communicate with funding institutions regarding deadlines, submissions, and other needs.
* Attend events as a representative for the University, UF Health, and/or UF Advancement.
* Develop and implement a comprehensive communications plan to inform stakeholders about the vision, mission, and values of the University, UF Health, and UF Advancement.
* Create value propositions that are consistent with the University, UF Health, and UF Advancement’s long\-term strategies, including Moonshots concerning health and wellness.
* Communicate the funding priorities and top initiatives of the requesting college/unit.


**Strategic Initiatives \& Collaboration**


* Engage colleagues across the UF Advancement community in an effort to build, maintain, and invest in professional relationships that allow for collaboration as opportunities allow, with a focus on corporate engagement.
* Partner with peers within the UF Health Office of Advancement in order to gain an understanding of their priorities and seek partnerships that positively impact philanthropic priorities.
* Attend team meetings as a representative of the Corporate \& Foundation Relations team.
* Contribute to a culture of philanthropy at UF Health through regular meetings with faculty and physicians to discuss funding priorities, provide insight on how to leverage corporate and foundation relationships, and

jobs.title: Director of Operations
jobs.description:
**Position Title:** Director of Operations

**Employment Type:** Full\-time

**Reports to:** Executive Director

**Salary Range:** $68,000–$76,000 per year, commensurate with experience

United Way Lamoille County is looking for a collaborative, organized, and mission\-driven leader to join our team as Director of Operations. This is a meaningful opportunity to play a central role in a community\-focused organization that has served the Lamoille Valley for 40 years.

We’re open to candidates from a variety of backgrounds — if you’re a strong organizer, a clear communicator, and someone who cares about community, we want to hear from you.

**WHAT YOU’LL DO**

In this role, you’ll help keep our organization running smoothly and our community programs thriving. Your work will span several areas:

* Oversee day\-to\-day finances, including budgeting, reporting, and working with our external accountant
* Support hiring, onboarding, and general HR functions
* Help manage office operations, vendor relationships, and facilities
* Track donations, assist with donor communications, and support fundraising efforts
* Help implement community programs and coordinate with partners and volunteers
* Identify ways to improve our systems and workflows

**ABOUT YOU**

You don’t need to check every box — we’re looking for someone with the right combination of skills, experience, and heart. You might be a great fit if you:

* Have experience in operations, finance, administration, or nonprofit work
* Are comfortable juggling multiple responsibilities and staying organized
* Communicate clearly and work well with a small, collaborative team
* Care about making a difference in your local community

**THE DETAILS**

* Full\-time
* Flexible scheduling for the right candidate
* Training available for the right candidate
* Compensation: $68,000–$76,000 per year, commensurate with experience
* Benefits include dental and vision, HSA contribution, EAP, and retirement savings access
* Based in Morrisville, Vermont

**HOW TO APPLY**

Send a resume and cover letter to hiring@uwlamoille.org with the subject line “Director of Operations.” We’d love to learn about you.

Applications reviewed on a rolling basis. United Way Lamoille County is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes candidates from all backgrounds.

Pay: $68,000\.00 \- $76,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Employee assistance program
* Flexible schedule
* Health savings account
* Paid time off
* Professional development assistance
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Morrisville, VT 05661

jobs.title: CEO
jobs.description:
**Overview**

We are seeking a dynamic and visionary Chief Executive Officer (CEO) to lead our organization with strategic insight, innovative leadership, and a passion for impactful growth. The CEO will serve as the primary architect of our corporate strategy, steering the organization towards sustainable success while fostering a vibrant culture of collaboration, accountability, and excellence. This role offers an exciting opportunity to shape the future of a mission\-driven organization committed to making a meaningful difference in our community and industry. The ideal candidate will bring a proven track record of executive leadership, business management expertise, and a deep understanding of nonprofit or corporate operations.

**Responsibilities**

* Develop and execute comprehensive corporate strategies aligned with organizational goals and mission.
* Lead and inspire senior leadership teams, ensuring effective management across all departments including finance, marketing, operations, and development.
* Oversee financial management activities, including budgeting, financial planning, and resource allocation to ensure fiscal health and operational efficiency.
* Cultivate strong relationships with board members, stakeholders, partners, and community leaders to support organizational growth and sustainability.
* Drive business development initiatives such as fundraising campaigns, strategic partnerships, and new revenue streams to expand organizational impact.
* Implement strategic planning processes that promote innovation, operational excellence, and long\-term vision realization.
* Supervise executive\-level teams in operational management, team supervision, and project execution to meet organizational objectives.

**Requirements**

* Extensive experience in corporate strategy development with a demonstrated ability to lead large\-scale organizations or nonprofits effectively.
* Proven success in executive leadership roles with strong skills in team management and business development.
* Deep financial management experience including budget oversight at an executive level and strategic resource allocation.
* Expertise in nonprofit financial management, fundraising strategies, and nonprofit sector operations is highly desirable.
* Strong background in strategic planning, operational management, and collaboration with board members or trustees.
* Exceptional leadership skills with experience supervising senior teams and fostering a collaborative organizational culture.
* Excellent communication abilities to engage stakeholders at all levels including board relations and community outreach.

Join us as we drive impactful change through innovative leadership! We are committed to empowering our leaders to make bold decisions that propel our organization forward while creating an inclusive environment where every team member can thrive. This paid position offers the chance to shape the future of an organization dedicated to making a lasting difference—apply today to be part of this exciting journey!

Pay: $52,837\.55 \- $63,632\.32 per year

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Executive Assistant to the CEO / Director of Digital Engagement & Fundraising
jobs.description:
**Executive Assistant to the CEO / Director of Digital Engagement \& Fundraising**

**Organization:** African American Youth Harvest Foundation (AAYHF)

**Location:** Austin, Texas

**Position Type:** Full\-Time

**Compensation:** **$60,000 annually** (Based on Experience)

**Reports To:** Michael R. Lofton, Founder \& CEO

**Join a Mission That Changes Lives**

For more than 20 years, the African American Youth Harvest Foundation (AAYHF) has served thousands of children, youth, adults, and families throughout Central Texas through education, behavioral health, workforce development, food security, youth mentoring, violence prevention, and comprehensive family support services.

AAYHF is also home to the **Harvest Trauma Recovery Center (HTRC)**—the first Trauma Recovery Center of its kind in the State of Texas—providing trauma\-informed services for victims of violent crime, domestic violence, sexual assault, child abuse, human trafficking, and community violence.

We are seeking an exceptional professional to serve as **Executive Assistant to the CEO while leading our digital communications, marketing, fundraising campaigns, and national outreach initiatives.**

This is **not** a traditional Executive Assistant position. It is a leadership opportunity for someone who thrives in a fast\-paced nonprofit environment and is passionate about making a measurable impact in the lives of others.

**Position Summary**

The Executive Assistant to the CEO / Director of Digital Engagement \& Fundraising will work directly with Founder and CEO Michael Lofton in managing executive operations while helping lead AAYHF's national fundraising and awareness initiatives.

The successful candidate will combine executive\-level administrative expertise with outstanding marketing, storytelling, digital communications, social media management, and fundraising experience.

This individual will help build national awareness of AAYHF's work while supporting campaigns that generate unrestricted funding for our life\-changing programs.

**IMPORTANT REQUIREMENT**

**This position requires demonstrated experience in social media marketing, digital fundraising, and communications.**

Applicants **without significant experience** managing professional social media platforms, marketing campaigns, and fundraising initiatives are respectfully asked **not to apply.**

**Primary Responsibilities**

**Executive Support**

* Provide executive\-level administrative support to the CEO.
* Manage complex calendars, meetings, travel, and scheduling.
* Prepare correspondence, presentations, reports, and executive communications.
* Coordinate Board of Directors meetings and maintain official records.
* Attend meetings with the CEO and provide follow\-up documentation.
* Assist with strategic planning and special projects.
* Maintain complete confidentiality.

**Digital Marketing \& Social Media Leadership**

Develop and execute a comprehensive digital marketing strategy across multiple platforms including:

* Facebook
* Instagram
* LinkedIn
* X (Twitter)
* YouTube
* TikTok (Preferred)

Responsibilities include:

* Creating compelling daily content
* Writing engaging stories
* Designing graphics
* Scheduling posts
* Monitoring engagement
* Growing followers
* Building community relationships
* Developing monthly content calendars
* Creating campaigns that inspire action

**Fundraising \& National Campaign Management**

The successful candidate will play a major role in helping AAYHF grow unrestricted funding through innovative fundraising strategies.

Responsibilities include:

* Managing GoFundMe campaigns
* Supporting online fundraising initiatives
* Assisting donor cultivation
* Coordinating crowdfunding campaigns
* Helping develop donor communications
* Creating compelling fundraising content
* Managing email campaigns
* Assisting with sponsorship outreach
* Supporting corporate partnership initiatives
* Helping coordinate fundraising events

**AI \& Digital Innovation**

Candidates must be comfortable utilizing Artificial Intelligence tools to improve productivity and marketing efforts.

Preferred experience includes:

* ChatGPT
* Microsoft Copilot
* Google Gemini
* Claude AI
* AI content creation
* AI research
* AI marketing tools
* AI productivity software

**Website \& Communications**

Maintain and update organizational websites by:

* Posting news
* Updating program information
* Publishing success stories
* Managing event pages
* Supporting SEO efforts
* Coordinating online communications

**Graphic Design \& Content Creation**

Create professional marketing materials including:

* Flyers
* Brochures
* Newsletters
* Annual reports
* Event graphics
* Sponsorship packets
* Presentation materials
* Social media graphics

Preferred software:

* Canva Pro
* Adobe Creative Suite
* Microsoft Publisher
* Photoshop (Preferred)

**Video Production**

A strong plus is experience producing and editing professional videos for social media and fundraising campaigns.

Examples include:

* Client success stories
* Testimonial videos
* Event recap videos
* Fundraising videos
* Promotional campaigns
* Short\-form social media videos

Experience with CapCut, Adobe Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, Canva Video, or Final Cut Pro is highly desirable.

**Public Relations \& Community Engagement**

Represent AAYHF professionally by:

* Speaking with donors and community partners
* Assisting with media interviews
* Supporting public presentations
* Coordinating community events
* Helping build national awareness of the Foundation

**Minimum Qualifications**

jobs.title: Fund Development Director
jobs.description:
**Position Summary**  
The Ensemble Theatre is seeking and enthusiastic, strategic and results\-oriented Fund Development Director to lead the organization's fundraising and philanthropic efforts. Reporting to the CEO, this position is responsible for developing and implementing fundraising strategies that support the Theatre's mission, productions, educational programs and long\-term sustainability.

The Fund Development Director will build and maintain relationships with donors, foundations, corporations, government agencies and community partners while working collaboratively with staff leadership, staff, and the Board of Directors to achieve annual fundraising goals. The ideal candidate is a strong communicator, results\-driven, relationship builder, and self\-starter with a proven track record of nonprofit fundraising.

**Essential Responsibilities**

**Fundraising \& Revenue Generation**

* Develop and implement annual and long\-term fundraising strategies to achieve organizational revenue goals.
* Identify, cultivate, solicit, and steward individual donors, corporate sponsors, foundations, and government funding sources.
* Build and maintain a pipeline of prospective donors and funding partners.

**Grants \& Institutional Giving**

* Research, write, and submit grant proposals, sponsorship requests, and funding applications.
* Manage grant reporting requirements and ensure timely submission of all applications and outcomes
* Pursue funding opportunities that support operations, productions, and educational programs.
* Monitor and evaluate grant outcomes, including tracking declined or lost opportunities and documenting insights to inform future funding strategies, when available

**Donor \& Board Engagement**

* Maintain strong relationships with existing donors, sponsors, and community partners.
* Collaborate with the CEO, Artistic Director, and Board of Directors to advance fundraising initiatives.
* Support donor cultivation, stewardship, and recognition activities.

**Events \& Community Relations**

* Plan and support fundraising events, donor engagement activities, and special campaigns.
* Represent The Ensemble Theatre at community, civic, and philanthropic events.
* Engage volunteers and supporters in fundraising efforts.

**Development Operations**

* Manage donor records, fundraising databases, gift acknowledgments, and development reporting.
* Prepare regular fundraising progress reports and revenue updates for leadership and the Board.
* Perform other duties as assigned.

**Qualifications**

**Required**

* Bachelor's degree or equivalent combination of education, experience, and results.
* Minimum of five (5\) years of nonprofit fundraising, development, or grant\-writing experience.
* Demonstrated success securing grants, sponsorships, and charitable contributions.
* Strong written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
* Proven proficiency with donor management databases, CRM systems, and Microsoft Office applications.
* Excellent organizational, project management, and multitasking abilities.
* Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast\-paced environment.

**Qualifications**

**Preferred**

* Certified Fund Raising Executive (CFRE) credential.
* Experience in performing arts, theatre, or cultural nonprofit organizations.
* Knowledge of corporate, foundation, and government fundraising practices.

**Working Conditions**

* Ability to work occasional evenings and weekends for performances, fundraising events, and donor activities.
* Ability to attend off\-site meetings and community engagement events as needed.

**Compensation and Benefits**

The Ensemble Theatre offers medical, dental and vision and vision insurance, a 401K savings plan with employer match up to 3% after 90 days of employment, paid time off and 10 paid holidays. Compensation is commensurate with experience.

**To Apply**

**Please submit a cover letter and résumé by email to:** ensemble\_resumes@ensemblehouston.com. Please indicate the position you are applying for in the subject line of the email. Applications will be accepted until the position is filled. No phone calls, please.

Pay: $90,000\.00 \- $110,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

jobs.description:
**PURPOSE**
The Director of Operations \& Administration is responsible for administrative excellence, governance support, compliance infrastructure, procurement operations, and workplace effectiveness across the organization. This position acts as a key operational partner to executive leadership, ensuring the systems, processes and administrative functions of the organization operate efficiently, professionally, and in alignment with organizational priorities. This role oversees board governance administration, records management, risk and compliance coordination, and procurement operations while driving continuous improvement across business operations. **KEY RESPONSIBILITIES** **Operational Leadership and Executive Support*** Lead day\-to\-day office operations and administrative infrastructure, ensuring efficient workflows and strong, consistent internal service standards.
* Coordinate organizational priorities, track deliverables, and support leadership through meeting preparation, reporting, dashboards, presentations and follow\-up management.
* Using data and operational metrics, identify opportunities to improve processes, strengthen accountability and enhance collaboration and performance across the organization.
* Lead or support other projects as needed.
 **Board Governance and Executive Administration*** Serve as the primary operational liaison to the Board of Directors and Board Committees, in support of executive leadership.
* Coordinate board and committee meetings, including annual calendars, agendas, board packets, presentations, minutes, resolutions, governance documentation.
* Maintain official corporate records and governance files while ensuring compliance with bylaws, policies, and nonprofit governance practices.
* Upload, organize, and manage board materials within Easy Board and ensure timely access to accurate information.
* Support board retreats, orientations, and special meetings through operational planning, logistics, and presentation support.
* Manage executive administrative functions, including CEO and COO meeting coordination, expense reporting, and executive support activities.
 **Business Operations, Procurement, and Vendor Management*** Lead organizational procurement and purchasing operations, including identifying purchasing needs, obtaining quotes, coordinating vendor selection, tracking requests, approvals, invoice review and follow\-up.
* Oversee management of vendor contracts and service agreements, monitoring contract and service timelines.
* Partner with department leaders and Finance to ensure purchases align with budget, organizational priorities, and internal policies and procedures.
* Own and continuously improve operations and administrative systems, workflows, and standard operating procedures.
* Map, streamline, and standardize recurring administrative processes to reduce duplication, improve turnaround time, and strengthen accountability.
 **Compliance and Records Management*** Lead organizational records management and retention, ensuring documentation is accessible, secure, and audit ready.
* Support risk management activities, compliance monitoring, insurance administration, and other organizational accountability requirements.
* Partner with Finance, Human Resources, Facilities, IT and program leadership to ensure administrative processes support compliance, risk management, and regulatory requirements.
 **Team Leadership and Workplace Experience*** Conduct coaching, performance evaluations, onboarding support, and staff development activities that strengthen operational consistency.
* Train staff on administrative procedures, systems usage, documentation standards, and workflow expectations.
* Ensure professional, welcoming, and customer\-focused front desk experience for clients, employees, visitors, partners, and board members.
* Foster a culture of responsiveness, collaboration, and operational excellence throughout the organization.
 **Qualifications*** Bachelor’s degree in business administration, public administration, nonprofit management, operations management, or related field required.
* Minimum of five years of progressively responsible experience in administrative operations, office management, process improvement, team management, vendor coordination, records management, or nonprofit operations.
* Extensive knowledge of nonprofit board governance, including fiduciary responsibilities, board and committee operations, bylaws, governance best practices, board development, policy administration, conflict\-of\-interest requirements, corporate recordkeeping, and applicable nonprofit regulatory requirements.
* Exceptional written communication skills with the ability to prepare professional board minutes, resolutions, governance documents, reports, and executive correspondence in a timely and accurate manner.
* Demonstrated ability to handle highly confidential and sensitive information with professionalism, discretion, and sound judgment**.**
* Certified Association Executive (CAE), Certified Nonprofit Professional (CNP), Governance Leadership Certification, SHRM\-CP/SHRM\-SCP, or other relevant professional certification preferred.

jobs.description:
**Title:** **Senior Director of Development, ASP of Chicago**  

Location: All Stars Project of Chicago  

Job Status: Full\-Time, Exempt, Hybrid  

Reports to: Vice President/Director, All Stars Project of Chicago  

Salary Range: $115,000 – $125,000 / annualized

*Are you looking to make a direct* ***impact*** *on youth and inner\-city communities by* ***developing*** *the lives of the people you reach?*

*Become a part of our* ***growing*** *national non\-profit team whose purpose is to bring out the All Star in everybody.*

**Job Summary**


The Senior Director of Development is a senior member of the All Stars Project (ASP) of Chicago leadership team at an exciting moment of transformation and growth — as we approach our 20th Anniversary in Chicago and launch a significant milestone fundraising campaign. This person will lead the planning and execution of cultivation and fundraising campaigns that reach hundreds of individual and institutional supporters each year, including our 20th Anniversary Campaign, which will raise $1\.5\-$2M in recognition of two decades of impact in Chicago.


In partnership with the Vice President/Director, the regional Chicago Advisory Board, and the Chicago fundraising team, the Senior Director of Development will be accountable for meeting annual and multi\-year fundraising goals for ASP’s Chicago region in pursuit of our mission to serve youth. The Senior Director of Development will be responsible for leading our annual Chicago fundraising benefits and building relationships with current and prospective supporters at the $2,500 to $50,000 level. They will be involved in major gifts work, lead our corporate fundraising strategy, and support regional Board and leadership development. The Senior Director of Development will be an internal and external leader, will support and develop fundraising processes and systems, and will be a dynamic and skilled manager of people.


This role will play a critical leadership function in strengthening and expanding ASP’s Chicago market presence, with the opportunity to grow revenue, deepen partnerships, and shape long\-term development strategy.


The Senior Director of Development reports locally to the Vice President/Director, All Stars Project of Chicago, and works closely with ASP's national Chief Development Officer. This position is a hybrid role and requires in\-person attendance in the ASP of Chicago regional office.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Serve as a senior leader within ASP's national development function, contributing to cross\-market strategy, best practice sharing, and organizational fundraising priorities — participating in national meetings, team activities, and strategy development as assigned by the VP/Director of Chicago and the Chief Development Officer.
* Meet or exceed fundraising goals for ASP Chicago each year (currently $1\.75M annually, with growth anticipated).
* Supervise, coach, and develop members of the development team, giving them ownership of key aspects of the Chicago fundraising operation, including training, conducting performance reviews, goal setting, and creating onboarding and development plans.
* Create and execute revenue\-generating plans and strategies with the Chicago fundraising team that benefit young people and deeply engage ASP teammates, volunteers, philanthropic partners and supporters.

* Lead and execute the strategic planning, execution, and performance of the All Stars Project of Chicago 20th Anniversary Campaign, ensuring alignment with broader organizational fundraising strategy and long\-term donor development.
* Partner with the VP/Director of Chicago and the Chief Development Officer to support the development of the Chicago regional Advisory Board, including producing and participating in board meetings, and managing the activity of two Board working groups.
* Partner with the Chief Development Officer, CEO, and other senior leaders to drive strategy, cultivation, and solicitation of principal and major gift donors.
* Co\-lead the Development School for Youth (DSY)Trustees, a dedicated group of corporate leaders, engaging champions in our programming and funding opportunities.
* Oversee the ASP of Chicago Annual Campaign, including strategy and execution of phone campaigns, mailings, and email strategies to supporters. Develop cultivation and engagement experiences for supporters in partnership with the national ASP Annual Campaign team.
* Deepen corporate partnerships through stakeholder meetings, both internally and externally, and by collaborating with regional and national development staff.
* Identify and pursue new and renewed opportunities for funding from private foundations.

**Qualifications**

* At least seven (7\) years of direct fundraising experience required, including executing personal solicitations of $25,000 or more and leading fundraising galas/benefits.
* At least two (2\) years of experience as a direct supervisor of staff.
* Proven track record of initiating, driving, and successfully completing assigned projects on time.
* Understanding of and experience with the Chicago philanthropic market preferred.
* Strong leadership and interpersonal skills and the ability to interact and communicate professionally with individuals at all levels of the organization.
* Strong verbal and written communication skills.
* Strong project management skills.

**Executive Assistant to the CEO**

**Part\-Time \| Approximately 15 hours/week**

**Pay Range:** $24\.00–$31\.00/hour

**Location:** Phoenix, AZ (primarily in\-office)

**FLSA Status:** Non\-exempt


About the Organization


Read Better Be Better is a nonprofit organization based in Phoenix, Arizona, founded in 2014 as a response to the state's literacy crisis. Our mission is to connect young readers and youth leaders to inspire a love of literacy and leadership. Our vision is a world in which empowered communities demand equitable access to foundational skills. Our values are simple – Be Excellent, Be Kind!


Job Summary


Read Better Be Better is seeking a highly organized, proactive Executive Assistant to provide focused administrative and communication support to the CEO.


This role is ideal for someone who enjoys creating order, anticipating needs, and helping a leader stay focused on high\-impact work. You'll help manage calendars, coordinate communications, organize priorities, track follow\-up items, and keep executive workflow moving smoothly so the CEO can focus on fundraising, strategic leadership, and external relationships.


This is a skilled, professional administrative role. Success in this role requires excellent judgment, discretion, organization, communication, and the ability to work independently while maintaining exceptional attention to detail.


What You'll Do (Top Responsibilities)

* Manage the CEO's calendar, scheduling, and meeting logistics
* Coordinate and organize executive communications, follow\-up items, and priorities
* Prepare documents, presentations, agendas, and briefing materials
* Maintain executive contacts and digital relationship\-tracking records
* Complete high\-quality administrative support that keeps executive operations organized and moving forward


A Day in the Life


A typical day begins by reviewing the CEO's calendar, identifying scheduling priorities, organizing inbox communications, and tracking time\-sensitive follow\-up items. Throughout your shift, you'll coordinate meetings, prepare materials, organize executive communications, update digital relationship records, and complete administrative tasks that keep executive operations running smoothly. Because this is a focused part\-time role, success comes from staying organized, exercising sound judgment, and prioritizing work that has the greatest impact on the CEO's time and advances Read Better Be Better's mission.


Key Qualifications

**Required**

* Three or more years of administrative support or related professional experience
* Strong organization, prioritization, and follow\-through skills
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills
* Sound judgment, discretion, and the ability to maintain confidentiality

**Preferred**

* Experience supporting a CEO, Executive Director, or other senior leader
* Experience in nonprofit organizations or mission\-driven environments
* Experience using CRM, database, or relationship\-management systems


Work Environment \& Schedule

* Approximately 15 hours per week
* Primarily in\-person at Read Better Be Better's Phoenix office
* A consistent weekly schedule is expected and will be established based on organizational and CEO needs
* Occasional evening or weekend work may be needed to support meetings or organizational priorities


 How to Apply


Apply online at: https://readbetterbebetter.applicantpro.com/pages/JobOpportunities/

**Application Process \& Timeline**


Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis throughout July. Selected candidates will be invited to a phone interview, with finalists participating in an in\-person interview in early August.


The anticipated start date is mid\-August, with onboarding and training taking place before regular work begins. Candidates can expect to hear back within approximately 2 weeks following their final interview.


Please have an updated resume ready to upload. If you need assistance with the application, contact us at **jobs@readbetterbebetter.org**.

*Read Better Be Better is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, non\-disqualifying physical or mental disability, national origin, veteran status or any other basis covered by appropriate law. All employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit, and business need. See the RBBB* *Equity, Diversity \& Inclusion Statement* *on the website.*

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer (COO)
jobs.description:
**Chief Operating Officer (COO)**
The Chief Operating Officer (COO) serves as a key member of the executive leadership team and is responsible for overseeing the organization’s day\-to\-day operations. The COO translates the organization's strategic vision into operational success by leading service delivery, compliance initiatives, quality improvement efforts, and operational growth. The COO works closely with department leaders to ensure exceptional outcomes for individuals served, employees, and stakeholders.
**Reports to:** **Chief Executive Officer**
**Key Responsibilities**:
* Provide leadership and oversight for all operational functions, including Home Health, Medicaid Waiver Services, Clinical Operations, Compliance, Human Resources, Quality Assurance, and Administrative Services.
* Ensure high\-quality, person\-centered services are delivered in compliance with agency policies, regulatory requirements, and best practices.
* Monitor operational performance, staffing, productivity, service outcomes, and key performance indicators (KPIs).
* Develop and implement operational processes that improve efficiency, effectiveness, and customer satisfaction.
* Lead continuous quality improvement initiatives and operational excellence efforts across all service lines.
* Ensure compliance with all federal, state, Medicare, Medicaid, Medicaid Waiver, and Home Health regulations.
* Maintain organizational readiness for licensing reviews, accreditation surveys, audits, and regulatory inspections.
* Oversee incident management, corrective action plans, risk management activities, and quality assurance programs.
* Monitor compliance with Indiana Administrative Codes (460 IAC), Medicare Conditions of Participation, and applicable employment regulations.
* Collaborate with leadership to identify compliance risks and implement proactive solutions.
* Provide direct leadership, coaching, and support to directors, managers, and department leaders.
* Foster a culture of accountability, collaboration, customer service excellence, and continuous improvement.
* Support recruitment, retention, workforce planning, succession planning, and employee engagement initiatives.
* Oversee staff orientation, education, training, and professional development programs.
* Promote organizational values and maintain a positive, mission\-driven workplace culture.

  

**Qualifications:**
* Bachelor's Degree in Human Services, Healthcare Administration, Business Administration, Public Health, or a related field required.
* Master's Degree in Healthcare Administration (MHA), Business Administration (MBA), Nursing, Public Health, or related field preferred.
* Minimum of five (5\) years of senior leadership or operational management experience in healthcare or related human services.
* Demonstrated success in regulatory compliance, operational leadership, quality improvement, and team development.
* Excellent communication, problem\-solving, and decision\-making abilities.
* Ability to analyze operational data and implement performance improvement strategies.
* Strong interpersonal skills and ability to build relationships with employees, stakeholders, and community partners.


**Physical Requirements*** Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer
* Frequent use of hands and fingers for typing, writing, and operating office equipment.
* Ability to communicate clearly via phone, video conferencing, and email.
* Ability to participate in extended virtual meetings and presentations.
* Ability to travel occasionally for leadership meetings, site visits, conferences, state meetings, or partnership development as needed.


While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly exposed to an office environment in which the noise level is usually low to moderate. The employee is occasionally exposed to outside weather conditions as well as Individual residences in which the noise levels are usually moderate.

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
**Company Overview**

Palmetto Surgery Center is a privately owned outpatient surgical facility operated by surgeons. Since 2000, we have provided high\-quality ENT and cataract procedures with an outstanding reputation among patients and referring physicians. Our center features five operating rooms and operates Monday through Friday, with no weekends or on\-call responsibilities.

**Job Overview**

We are seeking a dynamic and strategic Chief Operating Officer (COO) to lead our surgical center’s daily operations and long\-term growth initiatives. The ideal candidate will possess extensive management experience, demonstrate strong financial acumen, and excel in strategic planning and team leadership. As COO, you will oversee operational efficiency, drive process improvements, and ensure the delivery of exceptional patient care while maintaining profitability and compliance.

**DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES**

Employee shall actively perform the duties of a Chief Operating Officer (CEO) for the Company. Employee’s duties shall include coordinating and integrating services within the Company with physicians and business partners. Employee shall implement and ensure compliance with Company policies and procedures adopted by the Company’s Governing Board (the “Board”).

Employee shall also oversee: (a) staffing of the ambulatory surgical center; (b) human resources; (c) orientation, in\-service training and continuing education of all staff; (d) processes to reduce and control environmental hazards, medical waste and related risks; (e) policies to prevent accidents and injuries and maintain safe conditions for patients, staff, and visitors; and (f) development of annual operating and capital budgets, monitor revenues, billing, collection and operational and capital costs.

Employee shall continually assess and seek to improve the Company’s financial performance by designing operational processes to monitor financial performance through data, analyzing the data and implementing sustainable performance improvement.

Employee shall work solely on behalf of the Company and shall be subject to the policies, procedures and standards set forth by the Board. Employee agrees that the Company’s Board shall have the sole authority to establish policies, procedures and guidelines regarding the operations of the Palmetto Surgery Center (“PSC”).

Employee shall also:

1\) Review and make a list of all capital assets, develop a regular maintenance and repair schedule for all capital assets and make recommendations for the replacement of same.

2\) Implement and follow internal controls for all accounting functions as recommended by the Company’s outside independent accountant.

3\) Regularly, but not less than monthly, report to the Company’s Board regarding:

a. Number and type of procedures performed at PSC

b. Billing and Collections

c. Expense incurred (including payroll)

d. Staffing and HR issues

e. Patient satisfaction

f. Insurance costs and claims

g. Compliance with policies and procedures

h. Regulatory matters affecting PSC

i. Recommendations for improvement of PSC’s surgical efficiencies, patient safety, revenue enhancement opportunities and expense reduction opportunities

j. Quality initiatives and risk management

k. Credentialing of Professional Staff (excluding nurses) and Surgeons

l. Timely completion of and maintenance of patient medical records to include electronic health record (EHR) and compliance with HIPAA

m. Compliance with Stark III, the Anti\-Kickback Statute and the SC Provider Self\-Referral Act

n. Development of and implementation of a Corporate Compliance Plan for PSC

o. Inventory of supplies and drugs

p. Development and implementation of internal controls related to access to drugs by staff and surgeons to ensure accountability

**YMCA OF MOORE COUNTY**

The YMCA of Moore County offers an opportunity for a community\-focused leader to guide a strong, stable organization with deep local roots. With more than $1\.3 million invested in recent facility improvements and a debt\-free, financially sound operation, the YMCA is well positioned for continued success.


This is an opportunity to build on momentum. The CEO will serve as the visible leader of the organization, strengthening relationships with members, staff, and community partners while ensuring programs reflect local needs.


With opportunities to expand programming and deepen engagement, the next CEO will be able to make a direct and meaningful impact. Supported by an engaged Board and committed staff team, the CEO will help ensure the YMCA remains a vital resource for individuals and families across Moore County.

**Company Overview**

Skywood Recovery is dedicated to providing comprehensive, evidence\-based treatment for individuals facing addiction and co\-occurring mental health conditions. Located amidst serene woodlands, our facility offers a tranquil environment where patients can focus on healing and rediscovering joy in life. Our team is committed to delivering personalized care through innovative programs and compassionate support.

**Overview**

We are seeking a highly skilled and strategic Chief Operating Officer (COO) to lead the operational functions of our organization. The ideal candidate will possess extensive experience in managing complex business operations, driving strategic initiatives, and fostering a culture of excellence. As COO, you will oversee daily operations, implement operational improvements, and ensure alignment with our mission to deliver exceptional patient care and organizational growth.

**Duties**

* Provides executive oversight of all inpatient hospital operations to ensure safe, efficient, patient\-centered care. Oversee operational execution across assigned departments while maintaining alignment with organizational goals. Facilitates timely decision making, removes operational barriers, and promotes collaboration among leaders. Serves as administrator on duty in the CEO’s absence.
* Oversee daily operations including admissions, patient flow, bed management, census optimization, discharge coordination, staffing coordination, and operational readiness. Works collaboratively with department leaders to maximize efficiency while maintaining high standards of patient care and safety.
* Ensures continuous readiness for Joint Commission, state licensing, and other regulatory agencies. Oversees implementation of action plans, regulatory audits, and organizational compliance activities. Promotes a culture of continuous survey readiness.
* Provides coaching, mentoring, and performance oversight for assigned department directors. Establish operational expectations, monitor departmental performance, supports leadership development, and promote a culture of accountability, engagement, and continuous improvement.
* Develops, monitors, and reports organizational key performance indicators, including census, admission conversion rates, average length of stay, patient satisfaction, incident trends, productivity, and quality outcomes.
* Partners with the CEO and CFO to develop operational strategies, budgets, capital improvement initiatives, and resource allocation plans that support organizational growth and financial sustainability. Participates in strategic planning and implementation of organizational initiatives.
* Implement change management strategies to adapt to evolving industry standards and organizational needs.

**Skills**

* Proven expertise in operational excellence initiatives, process improvement, and large\-scale organizational change management.
* Extensive knowledge of inpatient behavioral health and substance use disorder treatment operations.
* Demonstrated experience leading multidisciplinary healthcare teams within a 24\-hour inpatient environment.
* Thorough understanding of Joint Commission standards, state licensing regulations, and healthcare compliance.
* Excellent verbal communication skills with executives and stakeholders at all levels of the organization.
* Ability to analyze operational metrics including census, admissions, average length of stay, staffing productivity, labor utilization, patient satisfaction, incident trends, and quality indicators.

**Chief Operating Officer**


This key position manages the business and operations functions including the overall administrative operations of the firm, reporting directly to the Managing Partner and the firm's Management Committee. jobs.title: Director of Operations
jobs.description:
Description:

Director of Operations


The Director of Operations will serve as the firm’s most senior operational leader. This position is hybrid/on\-site and can be based in Lansing, Michigan, Chicago or Charlotte, NC. The role is responsible for building and leading the operational infrastructure that enables the firm to scale efficiently while maintaining exceptional client service, strong compliance standards, and operational excellence. The role reports directly to the CEO and leads the firm’s operations, client service, accounting and HR functions.


This role develops the operational framework to support the firm’s strategy and is tasked with translating that strategy into clear processes, scalable systems, and accountable teams. Critical to the success of the role is ownership of operational initiatives so that members of the leadership team and advisors can focus on growth, client relationships, and strategic initiatives.


Why This Role Matters


As CG Financial Services continues to grow, operational complexity increases across teams, technology, and departments. The Director of Operations ensures the firm has the process discipline, systems integration, and accountability required to support that growth.


Core Areas of Responsibility


The Director of Operations will lead and oversee:

* Firm Operations – ensuring consistent, efficient operational processes across departments
* Strategic Execution – translating firm\-wide priorities into operational plans and measurable outcomes
* Service \& Sales Infrastructure – improving new client onboarding, service workflows, and client experience
* Technology \& Systems – optimizing CRM, performance reporting, portfolio management, trading technology, and integrated platforms across departments including managing the firm’s relationship with its fractional Chief Information Officer
* Compliance Support \& Risk Controls – maintaining operational standards that support regulatory requirements
* Process Improvement \& Scaling – standardizing workflows and eliminating inefficiencies
* Operations Team Leadership – developing and managing the firm’s operations, client service, accounting and HR functions.


A successful Director of Operations will:

* Establish clear operational ownership and accountability across teams
* Improve process consistency and service standards firm\-wide
* Reduce operational friction and cycle times in key workflows
* Manage and oversee implementation of technology and AI efficiencies that increase firm capacity
* Build scalable operational infrastructure to support future growth
* Strengthen reporting, KPIs, and operational decision\-making
* Implement operational efficiencies that increase margin
* Supervise the accounting team, through the corporate controller, to insure timely month\-end close, development of the budget, increase efficiencies, and production of variance reports
* Manage all elements of HR Operations including recruiting, employee review processes, insurance and payroll processes, and the HRIS.
* Lead Risk Management intiatives for the firm


Requirements:

Ideal Candidate Profile

* 7–10\+ years of operational leadership experience in wealth management or financial services
* Proven ability to scale operations in a growing organization
* Experience managing teams, operational systems, and cross\-department initiatives
* Strong strategic thinking combined with hands\-on execution
* Skilled at building processes, improving efficiency, and leading teams
* Can operate effectively in a fast\-paced environment with high levels of accountability


Our Core Values:

* Thirst for Knowledge: You are curious and creative, always building expertise and asking the right questions.
* Innovative Problem Solver: You propose solutions, lead by example, and follow through on commitments.
* Self\-Motivated: You are proactive, results\-oriented, and energized by new challenges.
* Authentic: You lead with honesty and integrity in everything you do.
* Positively Impact People: You are committed to growing those around you and striving for collective success.


We offer a competitive compensation package including base, performance bonuses, Safe Harbor 401(k), discretionary profit\-sharing, paid\-time off, medical, dental, vision \& other ancillary benefits.


Please submit a cover letter with your resume.

jobs.title: Executive Director - Signal-Return
jobs.description:
Are you ready to lead the creative resurgence in traditional printing arts? Our client, **Signal\-Return**, a Detroit nonprofit, seeks an experienced leader for its **Executive Director** role to advance its mission, strengthen long\-term sustainability, and making a lasting impact on Detroit's arts community.  

  

As the organization’s leader, you will have a unique opportunity to preserve and advance the teaching of traditional letterpress printing while supporting innovative programming and community partnerships. This hybrid role combines strategic leadership, fundraising, financial stewardship, revenue generation, program oversight, and community engagement.  

  

If you are a dynamic, collaborative leader with strong fundraising and revenue\-building experience, a passion for supporting the arts, and a commitment to mission\-driven work, we encourage you to apply.  

  

**Key Responsibilities**  

  

**Strategic Leadership:**

* Develop and execute a strategic plan in collaboration with the Board of Directors.
* Drive the organization's mission, ensuring it is effectively communicated and embraced by staff, volunteers, and the community.

**Fundraising and Development:**

* Identify and secure funding sources, including grants, donations, and sponsorships.
* Build a corporate giving program with benefits to drive sponsorship support.
* Create stewardship events to build relationships with donors, members, and community partners.
* Actively seek out new grant opportunities, write and submit applications/proposals, and interim/final reports.
* Manage donor database and acknowledgment process.

**Program Development:**

* Oversee the development and implementation of educational programs, workshops, and exhibitions.
* Foster a supportive environment for artists and printmakers to create and collaborate.
* Create collaborative opportunities for new partnerships outside the organization.
* Schedule and manage special visits (schools, business groups).
* Assist with workshops and special projects as needed.

**Operations and Administration:**

* Manage day\-to\-day operations, including budgeting, financial reporting, staff management, and payroll.
* Ensure the organization operates efficiently and in compliance with legal and regulatory requirements.

**Community Engagement:**

* Build and maintain strong relationships with the local arts community and cultural organizations.
* Develop outreach strategies to increase Signal\-Return's visibility and impact.

**Board Relations:**

* Collaborate with the Board of Directors to provide regular updates, seek input, and implement strategic initiatives.

**Qualifications:**

* Bachelor's degree in a related field or related job experience.
* Proven experience in nonprofit leadership, preferably in the arts or cultural sector.
* Strong fundraising and grant\-writing skills.
* Exceptional communication and interpersonal abilities.
* Knowledge of letterpress printing or a background in the arts is a plus.

**Compensation:**  

  

The compensation range for this position is $70,000 \- $100,000 per year, based on experience and qualifications.  

  

**About Signal\-Return:**  

  

Signal\-Return is a Detroit nonprofit dedicated to preserving and teaching traditional letterpress printing and building a community center for art, craft, design, and collaboration. Founded in 2011, Signal\-Return is leading the Detroit revival of letterpress printing. Signal\-Return's innovative and wide\-ranging programming—its hands\-on art workshops, Open Studio program, visiting artists, educational partnerships with universities, elementary and high schools, and collaborations with other arts and culture organizations—supports an ever\-growing and vital Detroit arts community.  

  

*Signal Return is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to a culturally diverse workforce. They are committed to providing an inclusive environment based on mutual respect for all candidates and team members. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, age, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, height, weight, marital status, gender identity expression, disability status, protected veteran, or other legally protected status by state or federal law.*
